key_insight: The paper argues that LLM-based theorem provers must evolve from solving predefined formal problems to acting as research agents capable of frontier mathematical discovery with rigorous formal reasoning.
engineering_relevance: For theorem-proving systems, this reframes the goal from proof completion on benchmark tasks to building interactive, tool-using workflows that support conjecture exploration, auto-formalization, and human-AI collaboration.
method: Systematically reviews AI4Math progress across datasets, auto-formalization, and proof synthesis.
Identifies limitations in current systems along five axes: datasets, relational structure, mathematical exploration, tool ecosystem, and human-AI collaboration.
Outlines a roadmap for research-oriented formal mathematics systems.
evidence_signal: The abstract describes a position paper and systematic review rather than new experimental results, datasets, or quantitative benchmarks.
watch_item: A key challenge is that frontier mathematics is open-ended and under-specified, so existing benchmark-driven theorem provers may not transfer cleanly to research-grade discovery workflows.
paper_title: From Solvers to Research: Large Language Model-Driven Formal Mathematics at the Research Frontier
